info.title: API.HttpService
info.description:
MightyCall API (Application Programming Interface) exposes platform features to be used within 3rd party apps.

Tell us how you use the API and which additional methods you need to build integrations with a 3rd party apps. Send us your comments and feedback at support@mightycall.com

get.description: This method can return: * list of calls based on parameters '''{?pageSize,skip,startUtc,endUtc,callFilter,customFilter }''' * information about a particular call based on it’s ID { id } ### Request headers: ``` Authorization: Bearer {auth_token} x-api-key: {api_key} ``` ### Response headers: ``` Content-Type: application/json ``` Parameters: /calls/{id}?pageSize=..&skip=..&startUtc=..&endUtc=..&callFilter=..&customFilter=.. If no calls found by specified criteria or there is no any call available for the authenticated user, an empty data will be returned as well as Http status 200 OK: ```javascript { "data": { "calls": [], "total": 0 }, "isSuccess": true } ```

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of calls to return Default is 10 items per one page.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: number of calls to skip.

parameters.name: startUtc
parameters.description: Start of the date/time calls interval at `yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss` format. By default is 'now - 1 day'.

parameters.name: endUtc
parameters.description: End of the date/time calls interval in `yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss` format. By default it is the current date and time ('now').

parameters.name: callFilter
customFilter
parameters.description: Calls search string.

parameters.name: extension
parameters.description: The extension number.

200.description: OK. In case of success, the response contains list of the available calls.

400.description: Bad Request. Some request parameter is invalid.

pageSize: The value 'invalidPageSize' is not valid for PageSize.
startUtc: The value 'invalidDate' is not valid for StartUtc.

get.description:
Returns call object by its unique identifier:
 
```
 GET /calls/[call guid identifier with dashes]
 ```
 ### Request headers:
 ```
 Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
 x-api-key: {api_key}
 ```
 ### Response headers:
 ```
 Content-Type: application/json
 ```
 
 ### Example:
 Request:
 ```
 GET /calls/7e734dc1-544d-4f04-8ccc-8bd5b197a88c;
 ```
 Response:
 
 Status: 200OK
 ```javascript
 {
    "id":"7e734dc1-544d-4f04-8ccc-8bd5b197a88c",
    "caller":{
       "name":"u2 u2",
       "extension":"101"
    },
    "called":[
       {
          "isConnected":true,
          "name":"John Doe",
          "extension":"100"
       },
       {
          "isConnected":false,
          "name":"u3 u3",
          "extension":"102",
          "phone":"+15551112235"
       }
    ],
    "businessNumber":"+15550152729",
    "dateTimeUtc":"2018-06-05T13:38:29.522Z",
    "duration":"23066",
    "direction":"Outgoing",
    "callStatus":"Connected",
    "callRecord":{
       "fileName":"records/T1U9YWttaWdodHljYWxfRjk1MjJFM0RFQjJGNDJBM0JDRDk2MjdCNTFBNDM0QTksT1U9WWFuZGV4LE9VPUluZnJhdGVsLERDPXJ1LERDPW1pZ2h0eWNhbGwsREM9ZGV20/7e734dc1-544d-4f04-8ccc-8bd5b197a88c.wav",
       "uri": ".../files/callrecord_20180605_1338_u2_u2.wav?recordid=[record-id]"
 }
```

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Call id unique identifier, in `Guid` format: 36 symbols with dashes, e.g. '7e734dc1-544d-4f04-8ccc-8bd5b197a88c'

200.description: OK. In case of success, the response contains list of the available calls.

* information about a particular call based on it’s ID {id}

### Request headers:

```
Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
x-api-key: {api_key}
```
### Response headers:
```

```

200.description: OK. Returns newly created call object.

400.description: Bad Request. Some request parameter is invalid: at least one of the input phone numbers is invalid number.

parameters.name: resolveContacts
parameters.description:
Determines if contacts should be resolved (resolveContacts=true) or not (resolveContacts=false). If it is set in true, request result will be populated with contacts object resolved in the contact book using client's phone numbers.
By default it is `false` (do not resolve contacts).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The count of rows to display in one result portion (page). If omitted, the default value is '100'. 
Should be a positive integer number.
Example: ```/journal/requests?pageSize=20```

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The number of page to return. Pagination should start from '1'. If omitted, the default value is '1'. 
Should be a positive integer number.
Example: '/journal/requests?page=3'

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description:
Specifies from where request coming from.
Special value 'all' could be used to point to the all origins.
When quering the requests and this item is omitted, requests of all origins will be searched (origin=all).

parameters.name: state
parameters.description:
Specifies the result state of the request.
Example: ```/journal/requests?state=connected```

parameters.name: isRead
parameters.description:
Filter read voicemails and unread voicemails.
This flag can't be used without ```inbound=voiceMail```
Example: ```/journal/requests?isRead=true```

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description:
Comma separated sequence of Guid
Example: ```/journal/requests?groupId=286e70d2-1722-48fb-bf08-91db248b8b91,38563f70-f64e-44da-9294-73f3c6239c2f```

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description:
Comma separated sequence of Guid
Example: ```/journal/requests?userId=286e70d2-1722-48fb-bf08-91db248b8b91,38563f70-f64e-44da-9294-73f3c6239c2f```

parameters.name: vmBoxId
parameters.description:

Example: ```/journal/requests?VmBoxId=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001,38563f70-f64e-44da-9294-73f3c6239c2f```

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Specifies type of request.
Special value 'all' could be used to point to the all types.
When quering the requests and this item is omitted, requests of all types will be searched (type=all).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Specifies The start date and time in UTC of the period to get records from. This is a string in the ISO 8601 format, example:
```journal/requests?from=2018-08-05T2:08:56.831Z```

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
Specifies the end date and time in UTC of the period to get records from. This is a string in the ISO 8601 format, example:
```journal/requests?to=2019-08-05T2:08:56.831Z```

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description:
Specifies one or several client phone numbers, comma separated if multiple, example:
```journal/requests?phone=+15554553000,+15554553001```

parameters.name: wfState
parameters.description:
Specifies the workflow state of requests:
```journal/requests?wfstate=Open,Resolved```

parameters.name: searchProperty
parameters.description:
Specifies the search param of requests:
```journal/requests?searchProperty=Client```
If this parameter is not passed, the search is performed on all parameters at the same time

parameters.name: businessNumber
parameters.description:
Specifies one or more business phone numbers without a plus sign, comma separated if multiple, example:
```journal/requests?businessNumber=15554553999,15554554000```

put.description:
Fully replaces a channel with specified channel id by new data in the request body. New channel ids will be created by the server, all the channels the contact had, will be deleted and new data placed instead of the existing channels. Create Auto Contact for old number and update Actual interaction.
### Request headers:
```
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
```
### Response headers:
```
Content-Type: application/json
```

parameters.name: contactId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the contact to update channel in. This string is 24 symbols of length. Should match the regular expression ```^[0-9a-fA-F]{24}$```

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the channel to be deleted. This should be a GUID string, e.g. 'b1774ee9-4f24-4490-abf6-a3e67576cd20'

parameters.name: channelModel
parameters.description: The input data object, containing channel properties

parameters.name: etag
parameters.description: Optional ETag of contact to be updated. Use it if you want to be sure that contact was not modified by other party (in this case you'll get "412 PreconditionFailed" response)

200.description: Created. In case of success, the response contains the message 'Created' and the channel's data that has replaced existing contact's data.

400.description:
Bad request. Returns 400 Bad Request if 
* contact id has wrong format
* channel id has wrong format
* channel data is malformed - for example, phone number or email address has incorrect format

403.description:
Forbidden. 
* Returns errorCode 'ContactsLimitExceeded' if max contacts count limit has been exceeded. You can not edit contact, if you have more contacts than your tariff plan allows to have. Use `contacts/limits` method to determine your current contacts limits.
* Can't delete CRM-related contact.

application/json.message: Contact Records limit exceeded! Please upgrade your subscription.

Specified contact was modified by somebody else during operation. 
In this case actual contact will be present in response body. To delete this contact use new etag value or just omit etag argument. Returns 412 with errorCode 'ContactWasModified'.

post.description:
Creates new channel for the contact. 'Channel' means some communication channel with your contact, e.g. phone number or email address. Fills ActualInteraction if it's null or empty.
You can create either phone number or email.
* to create **phone**, send phone request object, e.g. ```{"phone":"+18065551234"}```,
* to create **email**, send email object, e.g. ```{"email":"my@mghtc.co"}```,
* to add some labels to your channel, provide them as an array of strings: ```{"phone":"+18065551234", "labels":["primary number", "other info"]}```.
### Request headers:
```
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
```
### Response headers:
```
Content-Type: application/json
```

parameters.name: contactId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the contact to add a channel to. This string is 24 symbols of length and satisfy the regular expression ```^[0-9a-fA-F]{24}$```

parameters.name: channelModel
parameters.description: Channel data object, contains channel attributes.

201.description: Created. In case of success, the response contains the message 'Created' and the channel's data that has been added.

Bad request. Returns 400 Bad Request if 
* contact id is not valid
* channel data is malformed - for example, phone number or email address has incorrect format

Forbidden. 
* Returns errorCode 'ContactsLimitExceeded' if max contacts count limit has been exceeded. You can not edit contact, if you have more contacts than your tariff plan allows to have. Use `contacts/limits` method to determine your current contacts limits.

This API method provides the same functionality as the UI of the user's account.
The SMS will appear in the message history and will update SMS usage information.

get.description: Returns a list of contacts with pagination information. The handler will return existing contacts by default. If ?updatedSince query parameter passed through a response will include deleted records either with state: "deleted" property. If no query string filter is specified, the first page of the available tenant's contacts list will be returned back. This means that default paging parameters will be applied: **pageSize=100** and **page=1**. Therefore query string filter ../contacts will execute as: ../contacts?pageSize=100&page=1 ### Request headers: ``` Authorization: Bearer {auth_token} x-api-key: {api_key} ``` ### Response headers: ``` Content-Type: application/json ``` Standard contacts response is as follows: ```javascript { "data": { "pages": pages, //total number of pages satisfying the request "records": records, //total count of contacts satisfying the request, "currentPage": currentPage, //current page number (default is 1), "contacts": [ //array items: contact objects ] } } ``` To find some contact or contacts, this method call could be specified with query string search parameters. Search parameters should be added in the query string. If nothing found, 200 OK will be returned: ```javascript { "data": { "pages": 0, "records": 0, "currentPage": null, "contacts": [] } } ``` ### Example request: ../contacts/?name=John&name=Sparrow&phone=555&label=work&comment=my comment&tag=sometag&page=1&pageSize=100 This searches contacts which: * First name, Last Name or Middle Name has both substrings "John" and "Sparrow", * phone should include "555", * labels should include label's substring "work", * comment field should include "my comment", * tag field should include substring "sometag", * display first page of the results * page size should be 100.

post.description:
Creates a new contact from the data passed in the request body and returns created contact back. The contact fields that could be created are listed below.
 
 ### Request headers:
 ```
 Content-Type: application/json
 Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
 x-api-key: {api_key}
 ```
 ### Response headers:
 ```
 Content-Type: application/json
 ```
 
 Notes:
 * You should not pass contact id or channel id when creating data. Contact and channel ids are generated at the server side. If contact id or channel id is specified in the contact creation request, an error with http status ```400 Bad Request``` will be returned:
 ```javascript
{
    "errorCode": "ContactIdIsNotEmpty",
    "message": "You are not permitted to pass any contact id in this operation. Contact id parameter should not present."
}
```

 ```javascript
{
    "errorCode": "ChannelIdIsNotEmpty",
    "message": "You are not permitted to pass any contact id in this operation. Contact id parameter should not present."
}
```

parameters.name: checkIntersection
parameters.description: Flag determines if intersection check should be applied or not. If checkIntersection=true, contact won't be created if there is another contact(s) with the same phone number.

201.description: Created. In case of success, the response contains the message 'Created' and the contact that has been created.

400.description: Bad Request. Input contact's  data is not valid

403.description:
Forbidden. 
* Returns errorCode 'ContactsLimitExceeded' if max contacts count limit has been reached.
* Returns 403 with errorCode 'EmailLimitExceeded' if max email addresses count limit has been reached. 
* Returns 403 with errorCode 'PhoneNumbersLimitExceeded' if max phone numbers count limit has been reached.

delete.description:
### Delete By id[]
Deletes contacts with contact ids passed as input parameter. If there is no any contact with such id, 404 Not Found will be returned. Once deleted, the contact could not be restored.
```
DELETE ../contacts/?contactIds=contact-id-1,contact-id-2,...
```
where "contact-id-1,contact-id-2" is a comma separated list  of contact ids to delete./// 
### Delete by tag
Deletes contacts with contact tags contains tag passed as input parameter. If there is no any contact with such a tag, 404 Not Found will be returned. Once deleted, the contact could not be restored.
```
DELETE ../contacts?tag=tag_value
```
### Request headers:
```
Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
x-api-key: {api_key}
```
### Response headers:
```
Content-Type: application/json
```

parameters.name: contactIds
parameters.description:
The list of the contact ids to be deleted. 
            Comma-separated ids of the contacts to be deleted. Each contact id  should be 24 symbols of length and satisfy the regular expression ```^[0-9a-fA-F]{24}$```

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The tag of contacts to be deleted.

200.description: OK. On successful deletion, will return 'Deleted' message.

400.description: Bad Request. Request is invalid or request data is malformed, e.g. some contact id has invalid format.

404.description: Not Found. At least one of the contacts you specify to delete was not found in your contacts list or there is no any entry that matched a specified tag

This fully replaces existing contact data with a new one: the contact is get found by the id specified in the request url, than its data gets overwritten by the request body. 
            Note that channels array is also replaced by a new array specified in the request. So that, if some fields are omitted in your request body, those fields will be removed from the contact.
            In case of success, the method returns HTTP status `201 Created`, as well as the contact have been created and 'Location' header pointing to that newly created contact resource. 
            Created contact contains generated contact Id and (if they were provided in the creation request) channel ids in the Channels array.
            If you need to update just a part of data, e. g. one o several particular fields and to not change others, try to use the `PATCH ../contacts` method that allows to partially update the contact.
               
            Concurrent modification protection:
            multiple clients could interfere modifications of each other. To prevent issues related to concurrent modifications ETag field was added to Contact's model. 
            ETag is an identifier that specifies a particular version of a particular contact. If you want to modify or delete some contact and you want to be notified if contact is modified from the moment 
            of last read, you can specify ETag in If-Match HTTP header. If ETag is outdated, the server responds with HTTP 412 Precondition Failed status code. If you are not able to specify If-Match header, 
            you can specify ETag as argument of request or in contact's body.

get.description:
This method selects the first phone number, which contains the sequence of digits passed in the request url.
### Request headers:
```
Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}
x-api-key: {api_key}
```
### Response headers:
```
Content-Type: application/json
```

The format is
```
GET ../phonenumbers/ddddddddddd
```
where 'd..d' - from 1 to 11 digits. For example, if there are two phone numbers `+17775556678` and `+17775550078`, both requests:

```GET ../phonenumbers/555``` and ```GET ../phonenumbers/17775550078```
will return the first number '+17775556678':

```javascript
{
    "data": {
        "number": "+17775556678",
        "isEnabled": true,
        "isPrimary": false,
        "isTollFree": false,
        "isInternational": false,
        "smsMode": "Enabled",
        "callRecordingMode": "Both, OutgoingBeepDisabled",
        "description": null
    },
    "isSuccess": true
}
```
*Note*: the phone number subsequence should not start with '+' ('plus') sign.

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: The phone number to select or the sub sequence of digits the number should include.

200.description: OK. Returns object containing status `isSuccess` and a found phone number object.

400.description: Bad Request. Phone number or number subsequence requested has invalid format or not found in your business numbers list. For example, querying `/phonenumbers/$$$` will result this error.

Should be a positive integer number.

When quering the requests and this item is omitted, requests of all types will be searched (type=all).

from.description:
Specifies The start date and time in UTC of the period to get records from. This is a string in the ISO 8601 format, example:
```journal/requests?from=2018-08-05T2:08:56.831Z```

to.description:
Specifies the end date and time in UTC of the period to get records from. This is a string in the ISO 8601 format, example:
```journal/requests?to=2019-08-05T2:08:56.831Z```

phone.description:
Specifies one or several client phone numbers, comma separated if multiple, example:

```journal/requests?businessNumber=15554553999,15554554000```

Specifies one or several client phone numbers, comma separated if multiple, example:
```journal/requests?phone=+15554553000,+15554553001```

wfState.description:
Specifies the workflow state of requests:
```journal/requests?wfstate=Open,Resolved```

searchProperty.description:
Specifies the search param of requests:
```journal/requests?searchProperty=Client```
If this parameter is not passed, the search is performed on all parameters at the same time

businessNumber.description:
Specifies one or more business phone numbers without a plus sign, comma separated if multiple, example:
```journal/requests?businessNumber=15554553999,15554554000```

message.description: Error message

origin.description:
Specifies from where request coming from.
Special value 'all' could be used to point to the all origins.
When quering the requests and this item is omitted, requests of all origins will be searched (origin=all).

state.description:
Specifies the result state of the request.
Example: ```/journal/requests?state=connected```

isRead.description:
Filter read voicemails and unread voicemails.
This flag can't be used without ```inbound=voiceMail```
Example: ```/journal/requests?isRead=true```

groupId.description:
Comma separated sequence of Guid
Example: ```/journal/requests?groupId=286e70d2-1722-48fb-bf08-91db248b8b91,38563f70-f64e-44da-9294-73f3c6239c2f```

userId.description:
Comma separated sequence of Guid
Example: ```/journal/requests?userId=286e70d2-1722-48fb-bf08-91db248b8b91,38563f70-f64e-44da-9294-73f3c6239c2f```

vmBoxId.description:
Comma separated sequence of Guid
Example: ```/journal/requests?VmBoxId=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001,38563f70-f64e-44da-9294-73f3c6239c2f```

type.description:
Specifies type of request.
Special value 'all' could be used to point to the all types.
When quering the requests and this item is omitted, requests of all types will be searched (type=all).

from.description:
Specifies The start date and time in UTC of the period to get records from. This is a string in the ISO 8601 format, example:
```journal/requests?from=2018-08-05T2:08:56.831Z```

to.description:
Specifies the end date and time in UTC of the period to get records from. This is a string in the ISO 8601 format, example:
```journal/requests?to=2019-08-05T2:08:56.831Z```

phone.description:
Specifies one or several client phone numbers, comma separated if multiple, example:
```journal/requests?phone=+15554553000,+15554553001```

wfState.description:
Specifies the workflow state of requests:
```journal/requests?wfstate=Open,Resolved```

searchProperty.description:
Specifies the search param of requests:
```journal/requests?searchProperty=Client```
If this parameter is not passed, the search is performed on all parameters at the same time

businessNumber.description:
Specifies one or more business phone numbers without a plus sign, comma separated if multiple, example:
```journal/requests?businessNumber=15554553999,15554554000```

records.description: Number of requests records returned as a result for provided filter

from.description: Date is used as a start date of the time frame in which requests are searched

to.description: Date is used as a end date of the time frame in which requests are searched

API.HttpService.Models.Journal.CallersLi.description: Information about the completeness of the list of callers

countOfAvailableNumbers.description: Count of available numbers in list

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

startUtc.description: Start of the date/time voicemails interval in `yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss` format

endUtc.description: End of date/time voicemails interval at `yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss` format

tags.name: REST API Overview
tags.description:
## API Capabilities

MightyCall API (Application Programming Interface) exposes platform features to be used within 3rd party apps.

Current API version can be used to:
- Get list of calls in the account;
- Get information about a particular call;
- Get list of voicemails in the account;
- Get information about a particular voicemail;
- Get list of business phone numbers in the account;
- Get user status;
- Change user status;
- Get user info;
- Make an outgoing call via REST method;
- Get, search, create, modify and delete your contacts.

Tell us how you use the API and which additional methods you need to build integrations with a 3rd party apps.
Send us your comments and feedback at `support@mightycall.com`

## How do I get access to the API methods?

API methods are available at the following endpoint: `https://{prefix}.mightycall.com/{version}/api`.

To access an API method you need to provide:
- `{api_key}` - can be obtained in the MightyCall web app
- `{secret_key}` - can be obtained in the MightyCall web app or user extension can be used
- `{auth_token}` - authorization token which is generated based on {api_key} and {secret_key}
- `{version}` - API version. Latest version is "v4"
- `{prefix}` - environment prefix:
	- to test API methods or verify your integration use "sandbox"
	- to access API with your production account use "api"

{api_key}, {secret_key} and {auth_token} lifetime
- {api_key} lifetime is not limited, it works until you either delete it or re-generate in the MightyCall web app;
- {secret_key} lifetime is not limited, it works until you either disable it or re-generate in the MightyCall web app
- {auth_token} lifetime is 24 hours and then it required re-generation.

All API methods except Ping require both {api_key} and {auth_token} to be provided and give you account-level access. Ping method can be called without {api_key} and {auth_token} to verify that API is up and running.

## Integration principles and error handling

API should be accessed via secure SSL connection (HTTPS) using HTTP methods.

Each API method can return one of the following result codes:
- 200 - operation executed successfully
- 400 - wrong request or response is empty
- 403 - no access to the method
- 500 - operation execution failed

## How do I get a 'test' API Key?

You can use a shared test {api_key} to test API methods or verify your integration.
```javascript
"x-api-key": "d8bd6a52-6051-42cb-817e-57096094bb9d"
```

## How do I get a 'production' API Key?

To use API with your production account you need to get a production {api_key}.
You can get it in the MightyCall web app at https://panel.mightycall.com/mightycall/settings/api

Note, that API access is limited to certain subscription plan. Check https://mightycall.com/pricing/ for more details.

## Are there any API usage limitations?

Each {api_key} is granted with 2500 API requests per 24 hours period. When you reach this limit your API access is blocked and you need to wait till the next 24 hours to begin.

## What should I do if I have questions?

If case of any questions about the API you can contact us at support@mightycall.com

tags.description:
## Auth POST /auth/token

You can request authentication token in one of the following ways:

tags.name: WebPhone SDK Overview
tags.description:
WebPhone SDK can be used to integrate calling capabilities into a web app (ex. CRM system).

Current WebPhone SDK version can be used to:
- Use as inline script at external systems
- Accept and make calls via the MightyCall account
- Enable basic call control (ex: call hold)
- Receive call-related events (ex: call started)

Tell us how you use the SDK and which additional methods and events you need to build an integration with a 3rd party app.
Send us your comments and feedback at support@mightycall.com

## How do I connect WebPhone SDK to my web app?

WebPhone SDK is a JavaScript library which should be included into your web app code.

Choose one of the following ways to integrate SDK:
- inline mode;
- modal form mode;

#### Inline mode
Use the following html snippet:
```html
<script src="https://{prefix}.mightycall.com/{version}/sdk/mightycall.webphone.sdk.js">
</script>
<script type="text/javascript">
	var mcConfig = {login: "{api_key}", password: "{secret_key}"};
	MightyCallWebPhone.ApplyConfig(mcConfig);
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Init("myContainerId"); //container id to insert web app
</script>
```

#### Modal form mode
Use the following html snippet:
```html
<script src="https://{prefix}.mightycall.com/{version}/sdk/mightycall.webphone.sdk.js">
</script>
<script type="text/javascript">
	var mcConfig = {login: "{api_key}", password: "{secret_key}"};
	MightyCallWebPhone.ApplyConfig(mcConfig);
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Init();
</script>
```

To configure the JS library you need to specify your `api_key` associated with the account and `secret_key`.
By default, `secret_key` is an internal user phone number (ex: 100) obtained in the MightyCall web app. Key could be also used as the `secret_key` if sign-in by key is switched on.

If you did everything correctly you should be able to start using all SDK methods and events in your web app.

SDK usage example:
```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Call('+15551234567'); //replace with your phone number
</script>
```

## WebPhone Statuses

Before you start working with WebPhone you need to check it's status. List of all available statuses is provided in the table below.
To get the current WebPhone status use the following SDK method:

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	var status = MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Status();
	console.log(status);
</script>
```

 Status | Description
 ---- | -----------
 `inactive`  | WebPhone is not activated
 `ready`  | WebPhone is activated
 `call_incoming`  | WebPhone received an incoming call, however it is not answered yet
 `call_outgoing` | WebPhone makes an outgoing call, however is it not answered yet
 `call_started` | WebPhone processing a call
 `offline` | WebPhone is offline (not connected to MightyCall servers)

## How do I work with WebPhone SDK methods?

Example of a SDK method call:

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	var status = MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Status();
	console.log(status);
</script>
```

## How do I work with WebPhone SDK events?

Each event has 2 methods:
- `subscribe` - subscribe to an event
- `unsubscribe` - unsubscribe from an event

Event subscription example:

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	function webPhoneOnCallStarted(callInfo) {
		console.log('Call from:' + callInfo.From);
		console.log('Call to:' + callInfo.To);
	}
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.OnCallStarted.subscribe(webPhoneOnCallStarted);
</script>
```

Event unsubscription example:

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.OnCallStarted.unsubscribe(webPhoneOnCallStarted);
</script>
```

tags.name: WebPhone SDK Methods
tags.description:
#### Phone.Init
This method is called to initialize WebPhone SDK. However it is already included into the html snippet used to add SDK library to you web app code so explicit initialization is not required.

#### Phone.Focus
This method is used to focus on WebPhone window.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Focus();
</script>
```

#### Phone.Call
This method is used to place an outbound call.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Call('+15551234567'); //Replace with your phone number
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Focus(); //Focus on WebPhone window in case it is hidden by other windows
</script>
```

#### Phone.Status
This method returns current WebPhone status.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	var status = MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Status();
	console.log(status);
</script>
```

Possible WebPhone statuses:
- `inactive` - not launched
- `offline` - not activated
- `initiating` - initializing
- `registered` - activated and ready to use
- `busy` - in a call

#### Phone.HangUp
This method is used to end the current call.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.HangUp();
</script>
```

#### Phone.Mute
This method is used to mute the microphone during the call.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.Mute();
</script>
```

#### Phone.UnMute
This method is used to unmute the microphone during the call.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Phone.UnMute();
</script>
```

#### Call.Accept
This method is used to accept an incoming call.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Call.Accept();
</script>
```

#### Call.Reject
This method is used to decline an incoming call.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Call.Reject();
</script>
```

#### Call.Hold
This method is used to place the current call on hold.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Call.Hold();
</script>
```

#### Call.UnHold
This method is used to place the current call off hold.

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Call.UnHold();
</script>
```

#### Profile.GetUserStatus
This method is used to get current user status. Should be used like a promise:

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Profile.GetUserStatus().then(function(statusString) {
                console.log(statusString);
	})
</script>
```
`statusString` could get the following values: 
- `dnd` (not available)
- `available`

#### Profile.SetUserStatus
This method is used to set current user status. Method accepts desirable status as string and returns JS Promise. See available values below the code snippet:

```html
<script type="text/javascript">
	MightyCallWebPhone.Profile.SetUserStatus(statusString).then(
    function(result) {
        console.log(result);
    },
    function(rejectReason) {
        console.log(rejectReason);
    });
    </script>
```
`statusString` could get the following values: 
- `dnd` (not available)
- `available`

In success callback you will get result message with new status. In the error case, error callback will be fired with rejection message as provided above.

tags.name: WebPhone SDK Events
WebHook Notifications
tags.description:
MightyCall platform provides notifications for events (incoming call, outgoing call, etc.)
To send notification to external host, HTTP endpoints should be configured at MightyCall web app.
Note! Notification messages may deliver to external host in an undefined order. To process incoming messages `Timestamp` field could be used.

## How to manage url addresses for webhook notifications?
WebHook addresses could be defined at [the appropriate API page](https://panel.mightycall.com/MightyCall/Api#/webhooks).
You can define up to 10 addresses per mightycall account.

## WebHook urls requirements
Once urls saved the system runs a validation routine performing a `POST` request. The defined webhook url should respond with the status `2XX` (200-299).

## WebHook script example
1. Client saves the webhook address `https://my-crm.com/some-url/webhook/entry.php`
2. The system performs a `POST` request.

3. Example of a client script entry.php:
```php
//parse call-event params and write them into .txt file
$data = grab_params();
file_put_contents("log.txt", $data, FILE_APPEND);
http_response_code(200);
// grap params method
function grab_params()
{    
    $post = json_decode(file_get_contents('php://input'), true);
    $str .= "From : ".$post["Body"]["From"].PHP_EOL;
	$str .= "To: ".$post["Body"]["To"].PHP_EOL;
	$str .= "Call ID: ".$post["Body"]["Id"].PHP_EOL;
    return $str.PHP_EOL;
}   
```


Script answers with a status `200`:
When a call is received by the system, it is assigned a unique identifier which will appear in all notifications as `Body.Id`.

Here is a typical notification sequence for an incoming call:
- `IncomingCall` 
- `IncomingCallRinging (Extension 101)` 
- `IncomingCallStopRinging (Extension 101)`
- `IncomingCallRinging (Extension 102)` 
- `IncomingCallConnected (Extension 102)`
- `IncomingCallCompleted`

Here a call comes in to the system and there is a sequential dialing of two users with extension numbers 101 and 102. The user with extension 101 misses the call and 102 takes the call. Then the call ends.

Here is an example for the consultation call:
- `CallbackCall`
- `CallbackCallRinging (Extension 101)`
- `CallbackCallConnected (Extension 101)`
- `IncomingCallRinging (Extension 102)`
- `IncomingCallConnected (Extension 102)`
- `IncomingCallCompleted (Extension 102)`
- `CallbackCallCompleted`

Here the call comes through a callback form. The user with extension 101 picks up the phone and makes a consultation call to the user with extension 102. Then the consultation and main calls end.

Here is an example for the outgoing call to another user (colleague):
- `OutgoingCall (Extension 101)`
- `IncomingCallRinging (Extension 105)`
- `IncomingCallConnected (Extension 105)`
- `IncomingCallCompleted (Extension 105)`
- `OutgoingCallCompleted`

It is important to understand that for these and other scenarios, the `From`, `To` and `Id` fields inside the `Body` will be the same in all notifications related to the processing of the primary call. If an event is associated with a specific user callout (for example, `IncomingCallRinging`, `IncomingCallConnected`), its extension number will be contained in `Body.Extension`. `IncomingCallCompleted` can have a non-zero value for the `Body.Extension` field. If the field is filled, it means that the consultation call is completed. Otherwise this is a notification of the completion of the processing of the entire call. As an example, consider a call through a callback form, followed by transfer to another user:
- `CallbackCall`
- `CallbackCallRinging (Extension 105)`
- `CallbackCallConnected (Extension 105)`
- `IncomingCallRinging (Extension 101)`
- `CallbackCallCompleted (Extension 105)`
- `IncomingCallConnected (Extension 101)`
- `IncomingCallCompleted (Extension 101)`
- `CallbackCallCompleted`

As you can see there is a notification in the middle `CallbackCallCompleted (Extension 105)`. It means that initial user (who answered first) has hanged up the phone. Notification `CallbackCallCompleted` with no `Extension` says that the processing of the entire call is finished.

Additional information: 

In case of a call from the site, the name of the widget will be displayed in the `Body.From` field. The business phone number this widget is associated will be displayed in the `Body.To` field. 
In case of an outgoing call to a colleague, the `Body.From` field will contain the business phone number the call is associated, and the extension number of the employee will be contained in the `Body.To` field.

## Message format
New incoming call from external number {From} to business number {To}:
```javascript
{
  "Body": {
    "From": "+15559371200",
    "To": "+15550347567",
    "Id": "7df52a62-9db2-48cd-9ae4-7dec8203d822"
  },
  "Guid": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
  "ApiKey": "8a77c828-31c2-492a-b0c1-224cd94e9c3b",
  "Timestamp": "2017-05-25T08:58:10.5140000Z",
  "EventType": "IncomingCall",
}
```

#### `IncomingCallRinging`
Starting of incoming call with extension number {Extension}:
```javascript
{
  "Body": {
    "From": "+15559371200",
    "To": "+15550347567",
    "Id": "7df52a62-9db2-48cd-9ae4-7dec8203d822",
    "Extension": "103"
  },
  "Guid": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002",
  "ApiKey": "8a77c828-31c2-492a-b0c1-224cd94e9c3b",
  "Timestamp": "2017-05-25T08:58:15.5140000Z",
  "EventType": "IncomingCallRinging",
}
```

#### `IncomingCallStopRinging`
Incoming call to user with {Extension} is failed:
```javascript
{
  "Body": {
    "From": "+15559371200",
    "To": "+15550347567",
    "Id": "7df52a62-9db2-48cd-9ae4-7dec8203d822",
    "Extension": "103"
  },
  "Guid": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003",
  "ApiKey": "8a77c828-31c2-492a-b0c1-224cd94e9c3b",
  "Timestamp": "2017-05-25T08:58:30.5140000Z",
  "EventType": "IncomingCallStopRinging",
}
```

#### `IncomingCallConnected`
Incoming call to user with {Extension} started:
```javascript
{
  "Body": {
    "From": "+15559371200",
```

## Incoming call
Following messages will be generated for successful call:
- `IncomingCall` 
- `IncomingCallRinging` 
- `IncomingCallConnected` 
- `IncomingCallCompleted` 

Following messages will be generated for unsuccessful call:
- `IncomingCall`
- `IncomingCallRinging`
- `IncomingCallStopRinging`
- `IncomingCallCompleted`

## Outgoing call
Following messages will be generated for outgoing call:
- `OutgoingCall` 
- `OutgoingCallConnected` 
- `OutgoingCallCompleted` 

## Callback call
Following messages will be generated for successful call:
- `CallbackCall` 
- `CallbackCallRinging` 
- `CallbackCallConnected` 
- `CallbackCallCompleted` 

Following messages will be generated for unsuccessful call:
- `CallbackCall` 
- `CallbackCallRinging` 
- `CallbackCallStopRinging` 
- `CallbackCallCompleted` 

## Consultative call
Following messages will be generated for consultative call:
- `IncomingCallRinging` 
- `IncomingCallConnected` 
- `IncomingCallCompleted` 

## Transferred call
Following messages will be generated for Transferred call:
- `IncomingCallRinging` 
- `IncomingCallConnected` 
- `IncomingCallCompleted`

## Redirect to Voicemail
Following messages will be generated for call redirected to voicemail:
- `IncomingCall` 
- `IncomingCallRinging` 
- `IncomingStopRinging` 
- `IncomingCallCompleted`

## Group call
Following messages will be generated for group call:
- `IncomingCall` 
- `IncomingCallRinging` (for each participant)
- `IncomingCallStopRinging` (for each participant)
- `IncomingCallConnected` 
- `IncomingCallCompleted`

tags.name: API Changelog
tags.description:
### v1
- method to request of authentication token without `client_secret` parameter was restricted
- new method to request of authentication token by special key `secret_key` was added. (Key can be generated in the MightyCall web app)
- new REST method `status` to manage user availability was added
- WebPhone SDK events was extended

### v2
- configuration of `webhook` notifications. (Can be configured in the MightyCall web app)
- new REST method `makecall` to make outgoing call was added
- new REST method `profile` to get common user information was added
- integrating of WebPhone with `inline` mode
- WebPhone SDK events was extended

### v3
- call object has been changed in methods `calls` and `makecall`: new objects 'caller', 'called' was added, 'participants' and 'contacts' were removed. The fields 'from' and 'to' were replaced by 'caller' and 'called' objects.

### v3.1
- Introduced Contacts REST API

### v4
- Introduced new api urls without {prefix}.mightycall.com (where {prefix} is 'api' or 'sandbox')
- Introduced Journal REST API
